data.title: S&P 500 touches new highs after jobs report, no SCOTUS ruling on tariffs
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were higher on Friday, with the S&P 500 ((SP500)) reaching new intraday highs as the key jobs report came in line with expectations and the U.S. Supreme Court did not rule on President Donald Trump's tariffs.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) reached new all-time highs and was last +0.6% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.8%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.5%.

The indexes are on track for weekly gains after a week of losses.

The Supreme Court was believed to release its ruling on the legality of Trump’s tariffs on Friday, but a decision did not come in. The court could issue additional opinions in the upcoming weeks.

Also on focus today, the December nonfarm payrolls growth came in roughly in line, while the unemployment rate dipped to 4.4%.

“Friday's weaker-than-expected jobs report showed that hiring continued to slow towards the end of 2025, capping a tough year for the labor market. Although the market is still questioning the soundness of the jobs data given the recent disruptions from the government shutdown, throughout 2025, the labor market faced many challenges, including uncertainties from tariffs to artificial intelligence,” said Dennis Follmer, CIO at Montis Financial.

“Higher for longer on interest rates has been the name of the game for an economy continuing to surprise to the upside. As long as that economic strength continues, the market’s expectations for rate cuts are likely to prove overly optimistic,” Follmer said.

Also, the January consumer sentiment index also came in slightly higher than expected and higher than December, according to the University of Michigan Survey of Consumers.

Over in the bond market, Treasury yields advanced across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.18%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 4 basis points to 3.53%.

On geopolitics, Trump said he has called off plans for a second wave of military attacks on Venezuela, citing the release of political prisoners and calling it "a very important and smart gesture."

data.title: Torres introduces bill to ban insider trading on prediction markets
data.description:
Rep. Ritchie Torres (D-NY) is introducing legislation that would ban government officials and staff from trading contracts on prediction markets tied to government actions or political outcomes when they possess material non-public information or could reasonably obtain such information, the representative's office said on Friday.

The bill would apply to federal elected officials, political appointees, executive branch employees, and congressional staff. The proposal came after one person placed a bet of over $30K last weekened on Polymarket (POLYMARKET) that Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro would be out of office by the end of office by the end of January. A few hours later, the Trump administration captured Maduro, and the contract paid out $400K.

The timing of the trading immediately raised questions about whether the individual who bought the contract had inside information on the administration's plans. The legislation is intended to establish clear guardrails to ensure that public servants cannot profit from information gained through their official roles or from outcomes they may influence, Torres's office said.

"Prediction-market profiteering by government insiders must be prohibited — period," Torres said in a press release. "The blurred line between predicting and profiting does not simply corrupt markets; it corrupts the government itself, transforming public service into a private enterprise."

The Public Integrity in Financial Prediction Markets Act of 2026 is co-sponsored by a coalition of about 30 Democrats, including Nancy Pelosi (CA), Rashida Tlaib (MI), and Eugene Vindman (VA).

Melinda Roth, visiting associate professor of Law at Washington and Lee University School of Law, also commented in the release: "Event contracts are an emerging asset class in the growing prediction markets, and these markets must be free of any insiders (including government officials or employees, whether elected or appointed) using material nonpublic information to gain an informational advantage in this marketplace."

Other companies involved in prediction markets at Kalshi (KALSHI) and PredictIt. While the prediction market operators themselves aren't publicly traded, they have formed partnerships with publicly traded firms, including Robinhood (HOOD), Coinbase (COIN), News Corp.'s (NWSA) Dow Jones, and Intercontinental Exchange (ICE).

data.title: Waste Management hiked to Buy at UBS, anticipating 'return to capital returns' in 2026
data.description:
Waste Management (WM) +1.7% in Friday's trading as UBS upgraded shares to Buy from Neutral with a $260 price target, raised from $225, anticipating a resumption of stock buybacks that have been suspended since Q1 2024 will result in a ~2.5x increase in capital returned to shareholders and likely drive a relative valuation re-rating in 2026.

Waste Management (WM) recently announced plans for a 14.5% dividend increase in its 2026 dividend rate and a new $3B share repurchase authorization, superseding the previous $1.5B
authorization, UBS analyst Jon Windham said, adding that recently completed significant investments in growth projects and fleet upgrades will allow the company to focus on returning capital to shareholders in 2026.

Windham said the integration of WM Healthcare Solutions - previously Stericycle - is progressing better than expected, with consolidated segment margins improving sequentially each quarter since acquisition and target EBITDA synergies of $300M by 2027 within reach through cross-selling opportunities and efficiency improvements.

The WM Healthcare Solutions segment initially put pressure on consolidated margins but has since shown sequential improvement with each quarter, and although revenue growth in the segment fell short of expectations in Q3, Windham said the result was driven by isolated instances of price increase deferrals meant to retain customers during the integration period; the analyst forecast a 5% annual growth rate in revenue through 2027 as well as continued margin improvement for the segment.

data.title: Casey's General Stores' recipe for growth earns new bull at BofA
data.description:
As the third-largest convenience store operator in the U.S., the fifth-largest pizza chain, and the fourth-largest retailer with a liquor license, Casey’s General Stores (CASY) is well positioned to benefit from higher-margin foodservice sales. Combined with its strong Midwest footprint—an emerging hub for data center expansion— BofA Securities’ Lisa Lewandowski initiated coverage of the convenience store chain with a Buy rating.

The bullish rating and subsequent share price gain lifted Casey’s General Stores (CASY) to another all-time high.

While fuel sales generate 61% of Casey’s (CASY) sales, 70% of inside transactions did not include gas, suggesting that the store itself is a destination, Lewandowski notes, most likely for its pizza, as Casey’s may be one of the few quality food options in its mostly rural locations.

And its rural footprint gives Casey’s (CASY) a stronger market position as it is less expensive to build, buy, and operate in these locations. As well as expand. Seventy-five percent of towns between 500 to 20,000 people in its distribution center footprint do not currently have a Casey’s General Store (CASY).

In 2023, the company unveiled a 3-year strategic plan to increase store count, accelerate its foodservice operations, and enhance operational efficiencies. Accordingly, the company anticipates EBITDA growth of 8-10% annually, 500+ new stores, a mid-single-digit rise in inside same store sales, and flat to low-single-digit rise in same store fuel sales, as well as free cash flow of at least $1.25B through 2026.

“Despite low gasoline prices, muted fuel price volatility and cautious consumers, we think execution [of this strategic plan] is on track," she notes. 

Lewandowski’s Buy rating comes with a $700 price target which represents 29% upside from Thursday’s closing price.

data.title: Mesoblast rises as Ryoncil sales climb 60% in December quarter
data.description:
Mesoblast (MESO) ADRs added ~6% on Friday after the Australian biotech said that gross revenue from its cell therapy Ryoncil reached $35.1M during the December 2025 quarter, indicating a ~60% jump from the preceding quarter.

In 2024, the FDA approved Ryoncil as a treatment for steroid-refractory acute graft-versus-host disease (SR-aGVHD) in children aged two months and older, marking it the only mesenchymal stromal cell ((MSC)) therapy available in the U.S.  

In a regulatory filing on Friday, Mesoblast (MESO) disclosed that Ryoncil would undergo a pivotal trial as part of a second-line regimen for adults with SR-aGVHD, a market nearly three times larger than the pediatric population.

Additionally, the company announced a $125M loan agreement with its largest shareholder. The five-year arrangement is expected to lower its cost of capital and free up major assets, Mesoblast (MESO) said, adding that it has fully paid its prior senior secured loan thanks to the facility.

data.title: U.K.'s FCA to start accepting crypto applications under new regulatory framework
data.description:
The U.K.'s Financial Conduct Authority said in a recent notice that it will start accepting applications in September from companies seeking to offer crypto-linked services in the country.

The move comes after the U.K. government last month said it's preparing a broader regulatory regime for crypto firms, as it aims to bring key activities under formal oversight starting in October 2027. Draft rules mapping out proposed legislation for crypto exchanges and stablecoin issuance were published by the U.K. finance ministry in April.

The FCA's requirement also applies to firms that are already registered as crypto companies with the U.K. financial watchdog under its money-laundering rules, it said in a notice.

data.title: Homebuilder stocks surge after report on Lutnick meeting company executives
data.description:
Homebuilder stocks were trading higher after a media report about Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick meeting executives from major companies in the sector.

Lutnick recently met company executives as part of the Trump administration's latest efforts to bring down housing costs, people familiar with the matter told Bloomberg News.

The S&P 1500 Composite Homebuilding Index (SP1500-25201030) was trading +6.23% to 2,488.48 on Friday afternoon.

Top gainers in the sector included LGI Homes (LGIH) (+13.17% to $52.93), Hovnanian Enterprises (HOV) (+12.30% to $119.23), Toll Brothers (TOL) (+7.79% to $145.85), Lennar (LEN) (+7.56% to $117.83), Installed Building Products (IBP) (+7.43% to $294.24), D.R. Horton (DHI) (+6.45% to $155.31), PulteGroup (PHM) (+6.61% to $131.30), and Green Brick Partners (GRBK) (+5.95% to $71.41).

data.title: Quantum computing has advantages over traditional, but still in early innings: BMO
data.description:
• Quantum computing has advantages over traditional computing, but despite the investor hyper over the past 12 months, the field is still in the “early innings,” BMO Capital Markets said.
  • "Our belief is that quantum computing offers several advantages over existing computing and has potential longer-term use cases, particularly in scientific fields like chemistry and drugs, which will primarily be net new workloads,” analyst Keith Bachman wrote in a note to clients. “However, quantum remains very early innings, and more widespread adoption requires significant advancements in error correction, fault tolerance, and scalability.”
  • Bachman also noted that there are various existing quantum modalities already, but one of his sources believes there will be a convergence over the next five years. As such, the ion and atomic modality could wind up being the winner, “due to advancements in error correction,” Bachman added.
  • Related tickers: IonQ (IONQ), Quantum Computing (QUBT), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), Rigetti Computing (RGTI), Arqit Quantum (ARQQ), IBM (IBM), Microsoft (MSFT), Google (GOOG) (GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN)

data.title: a16z raises $15B in 2025 to accelerate tech advances, accounting for 18% of all venture capital in US
data.description:
Andreessen Horowitz, also known as a16z, raised $15B for an array of companies in 2025, which accounted for 18% of venture capital dollars allocated in the U.S. during last year, as the firm wants U.S. tech to dominate globally.

These fresh funds were directed towards American Dynamism ($1.176B), Apps ($1.7B), Bio + Health ($700M), Infrastructure ($1.7B), Growth ($6.75B), and other venture strategies ($3B).

"As the American leader in venture capital, the fate of new technology in the United States rests partly on our shoulders," said Andreessen Horowitz cofounder and general partner Ben Horowitz. "Our mission is ensuring that America wins the next 100 years of technology. That starts with winning the key architectures of the future – AI and crypto. It continues with applying those technologies to the key areas that generate human flourishing: biology, health, defense, public safety, education, and entertainment. And it culminates with the American government adopting these technologies to defend and advance American interests."

The venture capital firm has helped some of the world's top emerging artificial intelligence companies raise funds, such as Cursor, OpenAI (OPENAI) and Mistral AI. It has also backed hundreds of other tech and consumer companies, such as Figma (FIG), Okta (OKTA), Roblox (RBLX) and Airbnb (ABNB).

"If we fail to push the policies of the country in the right direction, America will likely lose its position as the global leader in technology," Horowitz added. "We have already seen the beginnings of this in both AI and crypto. And if America's position as the global leader in technology slips, it's not long before everything else slips with it."

data.title: Top small and mid-cap ideas with 27% average upside potential – BoA
data.description:
BofA’s U.S. Research analysts highlighted their top small and mid-cap stock picks for 2026, identifying 30 buy-rated companies they consider the best ideas within their “SMID cap” coverage.

To qualify for the list, stocks must have a market capitalization between $1B-15B, or belong to one of the major small and mid-cap U.S. indices, with liquidity of at least $20M per day. The curated selection carries an average implied upside potential of 27% to their 12-month price objectives.

The analysts see 2026 as a year where fundamentals and earnings will be the primary drivers of stock performance. Roughly 90% of the stocks on the list have experienced positive consensus EPS revisions over the last three months, with a median revision of +8%.

Looking ahead, the median next twelve months EPS growth projection stands at +23%, suggesting these companies are positioned for meaningful earnings expansion.

Among the highlighted opportunities, American Healthcare REIT (AHR) stands out as the stock with the largest positive EPS revisions in BofA’s REITs coverage.

Alaska Airlines (ALK) is positioned for a strong earnings recovery, benefiting from Hawaiian integration synergies combined with continued strength in premium revenues and corporate travel demand.

Also, shipping company Kirby (KEX) is expected to see earnings ramp from already record levels, driven by a tight supply/demand backdrop with additional potential upside from increased U.S. imports of heavier crude from Venezuela.

On the value side, UMB Financial (UMBF) offers above-average growth potential and net interest margin protection while trading at a discount to peers; Duolingo (DUOL) is flagged as having an underappreciated value proposition; and BGC Group (BGC) trades at just 15x next twelve months EPS despite analyst expectations for earnings to double by 2028, and stands to benefit from bank deregulation.

Here is the complete list:

  1. Alaska Air Group Inc (ALK)
  2. Alignment Healthcare Inc (ALHC)
